name: remuxing-to-mov
description: Losslessly remux broadcast and web video (.ts, .mpg/.vob, .mkv, broken .mov) into a QuickTime-ready .mov without re-encoding. One-command /mov shortcut picks the technique automatically (dual-track only when the audio needs it — E-AC-3 plays natively so it is copied single-track). Optional, opt-in QuickTime metadata embedding (proper mdta keys, no generic chapter menu). Five-rung escalation ladder (pure copy → PCM audio → genpts → elementary-stream timeline rebuild → scoped re-encode) with probe/diagnose/verify scripts, a dual-track PCM-access + bit-exact-original audio default, field-coded (PAFF) H.264 glitch repair, discontinuity (forward-gap) detection with an A/V duration-parity QC gate and an explicit audio resync, and decoded-pixel-identity verification of every output. Every codec/container fact verified empirically against a named ffmpeg version; the source file is never touched.
